definitions.es: ir; moverse de un lugar a otro
definitions.vi: đi; di chuyển từ nơi này đến nơi khác
definitions.en: to go; to move from one place to another

example.es: Voy a la escuela todos los días.
example.en: I go to school every day.

exampleSentences: Em đi chợ mỗi buổi sáng.
Anh đi siêu thị mỗi tuần.
Chị đi công viên chiều nay.
sentenceBreakdowns: Em (I) + đi (go) + chợ (market) + mỗi (every) + buổi (period) + sáng (morning)
Anh (I) + đi (go) + siêu thị (supermarket) + mỗi (every) + tuần (week)
Chị (I) + đi (go) + công viên (park) + chiều nay (this afternoon)
notes:
đi = “to go.” It’s the basic verb for moving from one place to another.

Use đi + place for destinations, and đi + activity/work for purpose.  

It also softens commands or suggestions at the end of a sentence (like “Go on” / “Let’s”).  
Close opposite for memory: đến = arrive.
notes_md:
**đi** = “to go.” It’s the basic verb for moving from one place to another.

Use **đi + place** for destinations, and **đi + activity/work** for purpose.  

It also softens commands or suggestions at the end of a sentence (like “Go on” / “Let’s”).  
Close opposite for memory: **đến** = arrive.
exampleSentencesTranslated: I go to the market every morning.
I go to the supermarket every week.
I go to the park this afternoon.

content.sentence: Chúng ta nên ___ đến công viên.

translation.en: We should go to the park.
